steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The claim has two parts: Lady Caroline feels bitterness AND she conceals it from her companions. The correct choice must show both her hidden inner state and a charming outer appearance simultaneously.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'lacks inner-outer contrast': Does not show both the inner bitterness and the outward concealment together. ✗
  • Choice B — 'lacks inner-outer contrast': Also missing the explicit pairing of hidden disillusionment and charming exterior. ✗
  • Choice C — 'contrast without naming bitterness': Shows a flicker of contrast (indignant vs. charming) but never names the underlying bitterness itself. ✗
  • Choice D — '"Her inside grew hoary with disillusionment, while her gracious and charming outside continued to make the world more beautiful."': Directly names the bitterness (disillusionment) and shows its concealment through the explicit inside-versus-outside contrast. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
